item.title: Your Interface Has Two Channels
item.description: This code would easily pass a cursory review: Then one day the endpoint returns a 500, flags becomes { error: 'Internal Server Error' }, no key matches a real option, and the server silently starts…
item.author: (Tomer Aberbach)

item.title: Travellers in a Foreign Land
item.description: A mental model for how to think about agentic capabilities

item.title: The Junior Developer Problem Is Becoming a Senior Developer Problem
item.description: AI is fundamentally changing how junior developers learn, but it’s also making senior developers a whole lot less valuable if all they’re doing is taking well-defined tickets and turning them into…

item.title: Wagtail as Django admin on steroids
item.description: Wagtail as Django admin on steroids.

item.title: Thoughts on Role Confusion
item.description: LLMs seem to almost ignore 'role' tags like <system>, <user> or <think>, and use the tone of text to infer roles. This explains a lot of jailbreaks.
item.author: (Giles Thomas)

item.title: When does data help automated agent engineering? · Andrew Jesson
item.description: When does data help automated agent engineering?

item.title: Security Is A Political Problem
item.description: Every now and then I meet a (usually) young and passionate security practitioner, uttering sentences like: “But this is just the bare minimum, we have to do (insert here: firewalls, phishing simulations, SAST, bcrypt, encryption at rest... pick one). We cannot work without it!“

item.title: The End of the Craftsman?
item.description: I noticed something a few months ago: I was talking to my colleagues less. Nothing was wrong. I’d have a question, describe it to an AI, get something useful back. Why pull in a human when the loop already closed? It took me a while to notice what that was costing.

item.title: Reinventing The Wheel, Now At A Bargain Price! – zwischenzugs.com
item.description: For twenty years, saying “don’t reinvent the wheel” was usually enough to win any argument about writing bespoke software. But that advice assumed two things: writing code was expensive, and importing code was cheap. AI and supply-chain governance are changing both assumptions.

item.title: Vibe Code Is Legacy Code Before It Ships
item.description: Vibe coding is useful and fast. Nearly a quarter of the YC W25 batch apparently has codebases that are almost entirely AI-generated. This is a giant leap in speed and capability. But there is a point…

item.title: luck surface area
item.description:
Your luck surface area is the amount of serendipity that will occur in your life.
Luck = Doing * Telling
Expertise is valuable and is created by doing with passion. Passion excites like-minded peopl
item.author: (https://blog.danwald.me/@danwald)

item.title: You gotta deal with the boring part first - Arnon Shimoni
item.description: Since 2024 the role of the CMO or marketer in a startup has really changed. Mostly, everyone has the same tools so everyone is producing the same slop at the same time. I can no longer remember times that I’ve read content that looks and sounds exactly the same. They organize cool dinners and then […]

item.title: How to Live Without Options - and Why It's the Key to Happiness
item.description: It turns out closing doors is the key to happiness.

item.title: Building a LoFi radio | Cieslak.dev
item.description: After listening to LoFi for quite some time, I decided to build my own radio station.

item.title: Introduction to making Makefiles
item.description: Introduction to making Makefiles

item.title: Debugging in the age of agents
item.description: Coding agents can hand you a confident, detailed, and wrong explanation backed by persuasive evidence. The craft of debugging - building hypotheses, gathering evidence, and knowing a system’s internals - matters as much as ever.

item.title: Still have spare tokens? Use /autoresearch
item.description: Everyone is tokenmaxxing around you, and you are standing there with extra tokens, not sure what to do with all these wasted resources. Normal. We were never blocked by the amount of code we could …

item.title: Slop Paralysis
item.description: A complete or partial loss of function while reviewing the output of a coding agent.

item.title: Building an FDE Motion
item.description: How to think about building an FDE org

item.title: RubyLLM
item.description: A single, beautiful Ruby framework for all major AI providers. Easily build chatbots, AI agents, RAG applications, content generators, and every AI workflow you can think of.

item.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom | TechCrunch
item.description: Named Jalapeño, the new processor was designed specifically for the unique needs of OpenAI's inference systems.

item.title: We’re making Bunny DNS free
item.description: Your DNS bill shouldn't scale with your traffic. So we removed query fees from Bunny DNS entirely. No per-request billing and no query limits.

item.title: Framework's 10G Ethernet module exposes USB-C's complexity
item.description: I've been following WisdPi's development of various 5 Gbps and 10 Gbps Ethernet adapters for the past couple years. They use newer Realtek Ethernet chips, which sometimes have performance quirks—most frequently encountered under Linux. In today's video, I tested the new WisdPi 10G Ethernet Expansion Card for Framework computers. It fits in any available Framework Expansion slot—even on the Framework Desktop. But Expansion Cards use USB-C for their connection to the mainboard—and therein lies the rub...

item.title: Founding a Company in Germany: €9,600, 152 Days, and I Still Can’t Send an Invoice
item.description: I started a company in Germany in late January. By late June I had spent 9,600 euros, registered two companies, and still cannot issue a single invoice of my...

item.title: Vulnerability Reports Are Not Special Anymore
item.description: We needed the insight and confidentiality to protect our users, but now that anyone can get the same results from LLM?

item.title: In memory of the man who put red and green squiggles under words - The Old New Thing
item.description: Starting in Word and expanding to nearly every other word processor, and even things that aren't word processors.
